creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) The AI gold rush is pulling private wealth into riskier, earlier bets
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: On a recent episode of Equity, we talked to Arena Private Wealth to explore a growing trend: family offices bypassing VCs to gain direct exposure to AI startups, turning them from passive investors into active participants.
contentSnippet: On a recent episode of Equity, we  talked to Arena Private Wealth to explore a growing trend: family offices bypassing VCs to gain direct exposure to AI startups, turning them from passive investors into active participants.

title: (No AI) Gemini is making it faster for distressed users to reach mental health resources
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK255_Google_Gemini_B_474198.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google says it has updated Gemini to better direct users to get mental health resources during moments of crisis. The change comes as the tech giant faces a [wrongful death lawsuit](https://www.theverge.com/tech/889152/google-gemini-ai-wrongful-death-lawsuit) alleging its chatbot "coached" a man to die by suicide, the latest in a string of lawsuits alleging [tangible](https://www.theverge.com/news/858102/characterai-google-teen-suicide-settlement) [harm](https://www.theverge.com/news/831207/openai-chatgpt-lawsuit-parental-controls-tos) from AI products.

When a conversation indicates a user is in a potential crisis related to suicide or self-harm, Gemini already launches a "Help is available" module that [directs users to mental health crisis resources](https://www.theverge.com/report/841610/ai-chatbot-suicide-safety-failure), like a suicide hotline or crisis text line. Google says the update - really more of a redesign - will streamline this into a "one-touc …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/907842/google-gemini-mental-health-interface-update)
contentSnippet:
Google says it has updated Gemini to better direct users to get mental health resources during moments of crisis. The change comes as the tech giant faces a wrongful death lawsuit alleging its chatbot "coached" a man to die by suicide, the latest in a string of lawsuits alleging tangible harm from AI products.
When a conversation indicates a user is in a potential crisis related to suicide or self-harm, Gemini already launches a "Help is available" module that directs users to mental health crisis resources, like a suicide hotline or crisis text line. Google says the update - really more of a redesign - will streamline this into a "one-touc …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The change comes as the tech giant faces a wrongful death lawsuit alleging its chatbot "coached" a man to die by suicide, the latest in a string of lawsuits alleging tangible harm from AI products. title: (No AI) Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Construction site with cranes and vehicles surrounding the footprints of buildings.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/10/Stargate-UAE-2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

An October 2025 image of OpenAI’s UAE Stargate data center under construction. | Image: G42

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has published a video threatening OpenAI's planned Abu Dhabi data center if the US follows through on threats to attack the country's power plants, as reported [earlier by _Tom's Hardware_](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/iran-threatens-complete-and-utter-annihilation-of-openais-usd30b-stargate-ai-data-center-in-abu-dhabi-regime-posts-video-with-satellite-imagery-of-chatgpt-makers-premier-1gw-data-center). The video, which was published to an [Iranian state-backed news outlet's X account](https://x.com/TehranTimes79/status/2039946692020092977?s=20) on April 3rd, says the IRGC will carry out the "complete and utter annihilation" of US-linked energy and technology companies in the region, before showing an image of [OpenAI's $30 billion in-progress Stargate facility](https://www.thenationalnews.com/future/technology/2026/01/26/stargate-uae-data-centre-to-cost-more-than-30bn-ai-minister-says/) in the United Arab Emirates.

OpenAI's overarching [$500 billion Stargate project](https://www.theverge.com/2025/1/21/24348816/openai-softbank-ai-data-center-stargate-project) includes investments from Oracle …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/907427/iran-openai-stargate-datacenter-uae-abu-dhabi-threat)
contentSnippet:
An October 2025 image of OpenAI’s UAE Stargate data center under construction. | Image: G42	

Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has published a video threatening OpenAI's planned Abu Dhabi data center if the US follows through on threats to attack the country's power plants, as reported earlier by Tom's Hardware. The video, which was published to an Iranian state-backed news outlet's X account on April 3rd, says the IRGC will carry out the "complete and utter annihilation" of US-linked energy and technology companies in the region, before showing an image of OpenAI's $30 billion in-progress Stargate facility in the United Arab Emirates.
OpenAI's overarching $500 billion Stargate project includes investments from Oracle …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The video, which was published to an Iranian state-backed news outlet's X account on April 3rd, says the IRGC […]
